ClientSummary.description: Compact client representation embedded in order responses.
ClientSummary.title: ClientSummary

ContactSubmissionCreate.description:
Wire payload accepted on POST /api/v1/public/contact.

Trim-on-input via `field_validator(mode="before")` so a user who pads
their handle with whitespace doesn't get bounced by `min_length`.
ContactSubmissionCreate.title: ContactSubmissionCreate

ContactSubmissionResponse.description: PII-free response — the frontend only needs the success signal.
ContactSubmissionResponse.title: ContactSubmissionResponse

PaymentClaimResponse.description:
Returned by POST /public/orders/{number}/claim-payment.

Idempotent: if the claim was already filed, the original
`payment_claimed_at` is returned and no new Telegram alert is sent.
Status stays `pending` — only the admin's manual verification
advances it to `paid`.
PaymentClaimResponse.title: PaymentClaimResponse

PublicFAQRead.description:
Storefront-facing — `is_active` is implicit (only true rows ship)
and we drop timestamps so the bundle stays compact.
PublicFAQRead.title: PublicFAQRead

PublicOrderStatusResponse.description:
Polled by the public payment-success page. Intentionally PII-free —
anyone with the order_number can read this, same trust level as a
Stripe/PayPal session reference.
PublicOrderStatusResponse.title: PublicOrderStatusResponse

ServiceSnapshot.description:
Frozen copy of the chosen service at order time.

`slug` and `title` are required; the others are best-effort and may be
None for legacy rows backfilled by the 0005 migration.
ServiceSnapshot.title: ServiceSnapshot

info.description: REST API for Nanoboost public site and admin panel.
info.title: Nanoboost Admin API

delete.description:
Hard delete — destructive, gated behind admin role (same posture
as game soft-delete; FAQs have no audit trail to preserve).

schema.title: Response List Admin Faqs Api V1 Admin Games  Game Slug  Faqs Get

post.description: V1: client-side logout (token discard). V2: Redis token blacklist.

get.description:
Unknown game_slug returns `{"faqs": []}` (not 404) so the storefront
can render the section unconditionally.

Returns a `Response` directly so cached requests skip Pydantic
re-validation on the hot path. `response_model` stays for OpenAPI.

schema.title: Response List Public Games Api V1 Public Games Get

post.description:
Customer signals "I have paid" for a manual-payment order.

Sets `payment_claimed_at` and pings the admin via Telegram so they
can verify the wallet/PayPal balance and flip status → PAID.

Idempotent — replaying the call returns the original timestamp and
skips the notification. Only PENDING orders accept new claims;
terminal-state orders return the current state unchanged.

Public surface, no auth — `order_number` is the credential, same
trust posture as the polling endpoint.

get.description:
Polled by the payment-success page after the customer returns from
the hosted checkout. Public — order_number is the only credential, same
trust posture as a Stripe/PayPal session reference. PII-free response.

schema.title: Response List Public Reviews Api V1 Public Reviews Get
